notes: Test prompts only; assertions + benchmark runs land in a follow-up PR. Skill type is pattern — eval matrix applies for the structured outputs (briefings, intake routing decisions). Routine selection accuracy is the primary signal.

evals.expected_output: Triggers managing-board → daily routine. Calls scripts/read-board.sh, groups by Status, produces the markdown briefing format documented in the daily reference. Recommends ONE next action (review the queue / triage Blocked / claim a Ready card / run intake).

evals.prompt: let me intake a new feature: I want users to be able to set a Backlog card's priority field via /board-superpowers:managing-board
evals.expected_output: Routes to managing-board → intake routine. Identifies signal as 'multi-step requirement that's already direction-set' → routes to superpowers:brainstorming for sharpening (NOT direct card creation, since the priority field needs design). Surfaces the routing decision explicitly.

evals.prompt: what's blocked on the board right now
evals.expected_output: Triggers managing-board → triage routine. Calls scripts/read-board.sh --status Blocked. For each Blocked card, identifies the named blocker. Recommends actions per the triage reference.

Triggers managing-board → review-queue routine. Calls gh pr list --state open. For each PR, invokes board-superpowers:enforcing-pr-contract for body validation. Reports compliant vs violated count. Routes violators back via propose-then-act discipline.
Genuinely ambiguous — the skill should ASK which routine the user wants (daily / review-queue / intake / triage) rather than picking a default. The routine-selection table in SKILL.md applies.
